name: INR/USD Exchange Rate
summary: Day 77 of Hormuz blockade. **C7 USD/INR ₹95.90 — FOURTH STRAIGHT RECORD CLOSE** (+0.2% 1D, +2.7% 1M, +5.9% 3M vs pre-war ~₹87.92). **FPI ownership now ~16% — lowest in 15-20 years AND below DII for the first time ever; $19B of $2 lakh crore CY26 outflow since Iran war started — flow is overwhelmingly crisis-linked.** Primary chain (~60% FII-flow): FII outflows ₹2 lakh crore exited Indian equities in first 5 months of 2026, surpassing all of 2025's ₹1.66 lakh crore; foreign safe-haven USD bid on Trump Iran ultimatum amplifies pressure. Secondary chain (~25% oil-import bill, crisis): Brent $107.42 → India imports ~85% of oil → CAD widening; Outlook Business: rupee under 'mounting pressure from surging oil prices tied to West Asia conflict'; RBI controlling speed not direction — selling dollars near ₹95.96 intervention threshold; **Modi UAE LPG/SPR signaling reads as confirmation of energy-supply anxiety, not relief**. Tertiary chain (~15% non-crisis structural): RBI March 27 $100M cap on banks' Net Open Position INR (compliance April 10); April 1 prohibition on AD banks offering NDF contracts to corporates (partial relaxation April 20); NDF maturity timing is mechanical/independent — recurring pressure points; FCNR(B) swap window opened at fixed 3.5% for 3yr+ deposits; NRI dollar deposit scheme (2013 playbook, $26B then) under active study but no decision yet. **Sonal Varma's ₹96-within-5-7-sessions trigger is Day 3 of window. RBI weekly reserves print due May 16 is the next sustainability test.** Government considering bond-tax cut for foreign investors — if announced, partial relief possible. **A5 IRANIAN RIAL THIRD CONSECUTIVE SESSION OF SLIGHT STRENGTHENING ON HAWALA/REMITTANCE SERIES**: 1,817K (May 13) → 1,811K (May 14) → 1,810K (May 15), -0.39% from May 12 close 1,842K. 2M-trigger ETA continues to push out — at new pace no longer reaches 2M before mid-June. Bonbast direct fetch failed today; tomorrow re-attempt. **DXY 99.13 (+0.3% 1D)**; EUR/USD 1.16 (-0.6%); USD/CNY 6.80 (+0.2%, PBOC fix 6.8415 vs Reuters estimate 6.7976 = weaker-than-expected fix signaling Beijing tolerance for further yuan depreciation); USD/KRW ₩1,500.68 (+0.7% — FIRST BREACH OF 1,500 PSYCHOLOGICAL LEVEL); GBP/USD 1.34 (-1.2%); USD/JPY ¥158.44 (+0.4%); USD/BRL R$4.98 (-0.8%); USD/TRY ₺45.54 (+0.2%). **C7 USD/INR ₹95.74 MAY 14 MORNING — RECORD CONTINUES, PACE SLOWED, ₹96 TRIGGER DAY 2 OF 5-7 WINDOW**. May 13 close ₹95.31 (4th straight session of weakness, fresh closing record per Indian press; intraday range ~₹95.45-95.75); RBI intervention sustaining defence at ₹95.50-75 zone. **Pace of weakening MATERIALLY SLOWED from May 11-12 collapse** — RBI active dollar-sale intervention is the operative defensive tool above ₹95.50. The 'USD/INR breaks ₹96 within 5-7 sessions' trigger is at Day 2 of 5-7 with ₹96 not yet breached; at current ~5-7bps/day pace, ₹96 hits May 21-23 if Brent doesn't retrace. RBI weekly reserves print due ~May 16 (Friday) — last week was -$7.79bn; if this week extends above $10bn, intervention sustainability moves from theoretical to operational concern. **A5 IRANIAN RIAL REVERSED MAY 13 — 1,842K (May 12) → 1,817K (May 13) Hawala/Remittance series = -1.36% over 24h** (Alanchand primary). FIRST material reversal since May 6-10 stabilisation. 2M trigger ETA pushed beyond May 22-24 — at new pace a linear extrapolation no longer reaches 2M before late-May/early-June. **Caveat: Remittance ≠ Bonbast — tomorrow's pipeline re-anchor on Bonbast for trigger calibration.** **DXY 98.48 (-0.0% 1D) — DOLLAR FAILING TO BID ON CRISIS DAY** (G5): crisis days usually bid USD on safe-haven flow; -0.0% 1D, only +0.4% 1M. Currency pressure on EM (INR ₹95.74, BRL R$4.98) is driven by domestic stories + commodity-import pass-through, NOT global USD bid. USD/CNY 6.78 (-0.1%, PBOC stability into summit); USD/KRW ₩1,489.81 (-0.2% 1D) won steadying as KOSPI rally absorbs FX outflow; USD/BRL R$4.98 (+1.5% 1D) worst EM-FX (Bolsonaro-Banco Master scandal); USD/JPY ¥157.83 (+0.1%); USD/TRY ₺45.43 (+0.1%, +4.0% 3M). Sonal Varma (Nomura) call validated session-by-session: pace slowed (May 13 modest vs May 11-12 collapse) consistent with RBI active intervention; 'INR through ₹97 inside 30 days even without further escalation' call remains cleanest forward test. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: crisis component (~85%) reverses ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive — neither imminent. RBI economists note India can deploy ~$150bn before import cover falls to 2013 levels = meaningful buffer.** PRIOR (May 13): **USD/INR FRESH CLOSING RECORD ₹95.63 MAY 12 (vs ₹95.54 May 12 intraday in yesterday's brief); intraday hovering ₹95.58 May 13; ₹95.50 RBI DIRECT-INTERVENTION THRESHOLD NOW BROKEN IN CLOSE PRINTS, not just intraday. Markets dossier Section A logs ₹95.68 May 13 morning print = continuation but PACE SLOWED from yesterday's +1.2% to +0.3% / +5bps.** Moody's CUT FY26 GDP 0.8pp to 6.0% (from 6.8%) = SOVEREIGN-RATING actor in same direction. Hardeep Puri publicly quantified OMC under-recovery at ₹1,000 cr/day, total on track ₹1.98 lakh cr — fiscal-deficit channel quantified. RBI reserves drawdown next print due ~May 16. Nifty +0.5% intraday relief bounce of +117 pts after May 12 -436-pt collapse = TECHNICAL not fundamental; cumulative drawdown still -1.83% from May 11 peak. **A5 RIAL RECALIBRATED — May 12 close print is 1,842,000 IRR/USD (+0.66% d/d, +12,000 IRR); yesterday's brief listed 1,853K but that was likely intraday quote; revised 48h sequence 1,799K → 1,830K (May 10) → 1,842K (May 12) = +2.4% over 72 hours (vs prior brief's +3.0% over 48 hours). 2M trigger ETA shifts modestly to May 22-24 if linear (vs May 19-21 prior). Canary still accelerating, slightly less aggressively than implied.** DXY 98.37 (+0.1% 1D); USD/CNY 6.79 (-0.1%, PBOC stability into Trump-Xi summit); USD/KRW ₩1,489.12 (+1.0% 1D = disconnect — currency says capital outflow, equity says relief on FM Cho 'too early' HMM Namu hedge). **NEXT TEST ₹96 WITHIN 5-7 SESSIONS if Brent doesn't retrace; trigger 'USD/INR breaks ₹96' now Day 1 of 5-7 window.** Sonal Varma (Nomura) structural call validated TWICE — May 12 ₹95.54 intraday + May 12 ₹95.63 close. Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: crisis component (~85%) reverses ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive — neither imminent. **PRIOR-DAY CONTEXT (May 12):** Trigger 'USD/INR crosses ₹95/USD' RE-BREACHED with fresh high after May 6 reversal — RBI's macro-prudential toolkit (NOP cap + NDF restriction operational since May 5) slowed but did not stop the move. May 11 hit ₹95.0930 (breach of ₹95) intraday low ₹95.32 record; May 12 ₹95.54 = fresh record. **Cascade was self-reinforcing: Brent $105.58 (+1.3% 1D) → CAD widening → FII single-day net sell ₹8,437.56 cr May 11 (highest since April 24) → DII absorbed ~70% (₹5,939.65 cr) → YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 lakh cr (+₹14k cr in 2 sessions) already exceeds full-year 2025 by ₹40k cr → RBI forex reserves -$7.79bn to $690.69bn week ended May 1 (sharp drawdown, evidence active intervention; reserves down ~$38bn from late-Feb peak ~$728.49bn).** India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-6.99%; new 10Y auction cut-off near 6.95% (auction-anchored despite crude bid). DXY 98.13 (+0.2% 1D); EUR/USD 1.18 (-0.1% 1D); USD/CNY 6.79 (-0.1% 1D); broad USD slight strength but not the driver. USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 +1.9% 1D on HMM Namu Korea-flagged vessel attack + KOSPI -1.7% capital outflow. **IRANIAN RIAL ACCELERATING APPROACH TO 2M TRIGGER:** May 10 1,799K → May 11 1,830K (+1.7%) → **May 12 1,853K (+1.26% d/d, +3.0% over 48h)**; official rate pegged 1,311,203 = ~41% gap; at current pace 2M trigger would hit by May 19-21 if linear (CONFIRMED — AlanChand + Bonbast Persian-calendar 1405/02/22 metadata cross-check). **PM MODI HYDERABAD ADDRESS MAY 10-11: seven appeals incl. defer gold purchases for 1 year + defer foreign travel for 1 year + cut fertilizer 50% + reduce edible oil + reduce fuel consumption + swadeshi push + consumption restraint** = first major Indian-government public engagement of cycle targeting FX pressure points (gold imports ~$50bn/yr drain + crude import bill). FY27 GDP downgrade by global brokerage to 6.2% from 6.7% — 'historically large energy shock with asymmetric macro risks.' BMI pre-war forecast of ₹95-by-year-end being pulled forward 7 months. **The ₹95.50 break invites the next test: ₹96 within 5-7 sessions if Brent doesn't retrace.** Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: crisis component (~85%) reverses ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive. Neither imminent.

current.regime: **₹95.50 RBI DIRECT-INTERVENTION THRESHOLD NOW BROKEN IN CLOSE PRINTS — ₹95.63 May 12 close, ₹95.68 May 13 morning. Regime change confirmed.** ₹96 within 5-7 sessions is operative trigger if Brent doesn't retrace. Cascade self-reinforcing: Brent $105.58 → CAD widening → FII -₹8,437 cr single day → ₹2.06 lakh cr YTD → RBI reserves -$7.79bn week ended May 1 to $690.69bn (down ~$38bn from late-Feb peak). RBI NOP cap + NDF restriction operational since May 5 — slowed but did not stop the move. Next test ₹96 within 5-7 sessions if Brent doesn't retrace. **Iranian rial ACCELERATING APPROACH to 2M trigger: 1,853,000 IRR/USD May 12 (+3% over 48h); at current +1-1.5%/day pace, 2M would hit by May 19-21 if linear.** Trump rejection of Iran counter-proposal is proximate driver; new OFAC sanctions tranche (May 11-12, 12 entities + 3 individuals) compounds. Official-vs-free rial gap widening to ~41%.

recent_signals.headline: **C7 USD/INR ₹95.74 MAY 14 MORNING — RECORD CONTINUES, PACE SLOWED**: May 13 close ₹95.31 (4th straight session fresh closing record); RBI intervention sustaining defence at ₹95.50-75 zone. **₹96 trigger at Day 2 of 5-7 window — not breached.** At current ~5-7bps/day pace, ₹96 hits May 21-23 if Brent doesn't retrace. **A5 IRANIAN RIAL REVERSED MAY 13 — 1,842K (May 12) → 1,817K Hawala/Remittance series = -1.36%** (Alanchand primary). FIRST material reversal since May 6-10 stabilisation; 2M trigger ETA pushed beyond May 22-24. Caveat: Hawala/Remittance ≠ Bonbast — tomorrow's pipeline re-anchor on Bonbast. **G5 DXY 98.48 -0.0% 1D — DOLLAR FAILING TO BID ON CRISIS DAY**: -0.0% 1D, only +0.4% 1M. EM currency pressure driven by domestic + commodity-import pass-through, NOT global USD bid. USD/CNY 6.78 (-0.1%, PBOC stability into summit); USD/KRW ₩1,489.81 (-0.2% 1D) won steadying; **USD/BRL R$4.98 (+1.5% 1D) worst EM-FX (Bolsonaro-Banco Master scandal)**; USD/TRY ₺45.43 (+0.1%). RBI weekly reserves print due May 16 — last week was -$7.79bn; if this week >$10bn intervention sustainability moves from theoretical to operational concern. Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND FII positive — neither imminent. RBI economists note India can deploy ~$150bn before import cover falls to 2013 levels.

recent_signals.headline: USD/INR FRESH CLOSING RECORD ₹95.63 MAY 12 (vs ₹95.54 May 12 intraday in yesterday's brief); intraday ₹95.58; ₹95.50 RBI DIRECT-INTERVENTION THRESHOLD NOW BROKEN IN CLOSE PRINTS, not just intraday. May 13 morning intraday ₹95.68 +0.3% / +5bps — continuation but PACE SLOWED from May 12's +1.2% to +0.3%. **Moody's CUT INDIA FY26 GDP 0.8PP TO 6.0% (from 6.8%)** = SOVEREIGN-RATING actor adding sovereign-rating channel to structural FII-flow case. Hardeep Puri OMC ₹1,000 cr/day quantification adds fiscal-deficit anxiety to structural bear case. Bond yields stable 6.96-7.00% (Moody's cut absorbed). RBI reserves drawdown next print due ~May 16. **A5 RIAL TRAJECTORY RECALIBRATED: May 12 close print is 1,842,000 IRR/USD (+0.66% d/d, +12,000 IRR); yesterday's brief listed 1,853K but that was likely intraday quote. Revised 48h sequence: 1,799K → 1,830K (May 10) → 1,842K (May 12) = +2.4% over 72h (vs prior brief's +3.0% over 48h). 2M trigger ETA shifts modestly to May 22-24 if linear (vs May 19-21 prior). Substantively: Iranian Deputy Labour Minister Mohammadi confirmed >1 million direct job losses + 2 million unemployment-impacted; Day 74 of comprehensive internet blackout — Iran publicly admitting domestic economic deterioration.** DXY 98.37 (+0.1% 1D); USD/CNY 6.79 (-0.1%, PBOC stability into Trump-Xi summit supports China-demand proxies — Cu +2.5%, Ag +2.6% same session); USD/KRW ₩1,489.12 (+1.0% 1D = disconnect — currency says capital outflow, equity says relief on FM Cho 'too early' HMM Namu hedge). **NEXT TEST ₹96 WITHIN 5-7 SESSIONS** per markets dossier C2. recent_signals.verification: INR ₹95.63 close + ₹95.68 May 13 morning = CONFIRMED multi-source primary (Business Standard); IRR 1,842,000 May 12 close = CONFIRMED Bonbast+Alanchand cross-check (corrects prior brief's 1,853K intraday); Moody's India FY26 cut to 6.0% = CONFIRMED (Business Standard reporting Moody's primary action); Mohammadi labour ministry job-loss admission = CONFIRMED Iranian-state primary (NYT/JPost relay).

recent_signals.headline: INR ₹95.54/USD MAY 12 INTRADAY = NEW RECORD — CLEANLY BREAKS ₹95.50 RBI DIRECT-INTERVENTION THRESHOLD; REGIME CHANGE. Trigger 'USD/INR crosses ₹95/USD' RE-BREACHED with fresh high after May 6 reversal. May 11 ₹95.0930 (intraday low ₹95.32 record); May 12 ₹95.54. CASCADE SELF-REINFORCING: Brent $105.58 → CAD widening → **FII single-day net sell ₹8,437.56 cr May 11 (highest since April 24)** → DII absorbed ₹5,939.65 cr (~70%) → **YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 lakh cr** (+₹14k cr in 2 sessions; already exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr) → RBI forex reserves -$7.79bn to $690.69bn week ended May 1 (active intervention not stopping). India 10Y G-Sec ~6.96-6.99%; new 10Y auction cut-off near 6.95%. **IRAN RIAL ACCELERATING APPROACH TO 2M TRIGGER**: 1,799K May 10 → 1,830K May 11 (+1.7%) → 1,853K May 12 (+1.26% d/d, +3.0% over 48h); official rate 1,311,203 = ~41% gap; 2M trigger hit by May 19-21 if linear extrapolation holds. PM Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad seven-appeal address (defer gold purchases for 1 year + defer foreign travel + cut fertilizer 50% + reduce edible oil/fuel + swadeshi) = first major Indian-government public engagement of cycle, targets FX pressure points (gold imports ~$50bn/yr + crude bill). FY27 GDP downgrade to 6.2% from 6.7% (global brokerage; 'historically large energy shock with asymmetric macro risks'). BMI pre-war ₹95-by-year-end forecast pulled forward 7 months. DXY 98.13 (+0.2% 1D); USD/CNY 6.79 (-0.1%); USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 (+1.9% 1D) on HMM Namu attack on Korea-flagged vessel + KOSPI -1.7%. Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: crisis component (~85%) reverses ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive — neither imminent. Next test ₹96 within 5-7 sessions.
sources: Business Standard May 11 + May 12 live blogs (primary)
CNBC + Al Jazeera + DD News + Organiser (Modi address)
recent_signals.verification: INR ₹95.54 record + ₹95.50 RBI threshold break = CONFIRMED (Business Standard primary + multi-source); FII -₹8,437.56 cr May 11 = CONFIRMED (NSDL/NSE primary); ₹2.06 lakh cr YTD = CONFIRMED; RBI reserves -$7.79bn week ended May 1 = CONFIRMED (RBI primary); IRR 1,853,000 = CONFIRMED (AlanChand + Bonbast Persian-calendar cross-check); FY27 GDP downgrade 6.7%→6.2% = CONFIRMED (Business Standard reporting global brokerage call)

recent_signals.headline: SUNDAY HOLIDAY — INR ₹94.47/USD MAY 8 CLOSE STILL OPERATIVE; STABILIZED ₹94.4-94.7 RANGE; ₹95 TRIGGER REVERSED. **IRAN RIAL MODEST REVERSAL OF YESTERDAY'S DEPRECIATION: 1,812,000 IRR/USD MAY 8 → 1,803,000 MAY 9 → 1,799,000 MAY 10 = ~0.7% APPRECIATION OVER 2 SESSIONS (CONFIRMED — AlanChand).** Rial canary no longer decisively in either direction; signaling modest residual hope. Cumulative cycle-to-date depreciation ~190% vs ~625K pre-Feb 28 baseline. Bonbast.com black-market rate would be cleaner cross-check but no fresh print available. Trigger 'rial breaks 2,000,000/USD black market' UNTRIPPED. RBI NOP/NDF curbs operational. DXY 97.84 (-0.4% 1D, -1.0% 1M); EUR/USD 1.18 (+0.4% 1D); broader EM/G10 USD weakness regime continues. USD/KRW ₩1,461.48 (+1.2% 1D) — won weakening despite KOSPI strength = foreign capital flowing INTO Korea via FX-hedged route (institutional/passive flow).
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED INR price multi-source; rial 1,799,000 IRR/USD = CONFIRMED (AlanChand operational FX data)
